---

# MMS - Metro Mileage Management System - Epic Breakdown

## Overview

This document provides the complete epic and story breakdown for MMS, decomposing the requirements from the PRD, UX Design, and Architecture into implementable stories organized by user value delivery.

**Project Context:**
- 12-week demo timeline
- 15-minute presentation to win competitive bid
- Primary differentiator: Km balancing as primary objective with visible justifications

---

## Requirements Inventory

### Functional Requirements

| ID | Module | Requirement | Priority |
|----|--------|-------------|----------|
| FR-1.1 | Dashboard | Display fleet summary KPIs (total, available, maintenance, variance) | P0 |
| FR-1.2 | Dashboard | Show variance indicator with traffic-light (вүӨ10% green, 10-20% yellow, >20% red) | P0 |
| FR-1.3 | Dashboard | Display depot capacity cards | P1 |
| FR-1.4 | Dashboard | Show maintenance alerts (upcoming PM/HM in 7 days) | P1 |
| FR-1.5 | Dashboard | Enable drill-down navigation | P0 |
| FR-2.1 | Map | Display train positions on map | P0 |
| FR-2.2 | Map | Color-code markers by status (available=green, in-service=blue, maintenance=red) | P0 |
| FR-2.3 | Map | Show depot locations with capacity indicator | P1 |
| FR-2.4 | Map | Enable train selection (click marker shows train card) | P0 |
| FR-2.5 | Map | Auto-refresh positions every 30 seconds | P1 |
| FR-3.1 | Allocation | List pending services | P0 |
| FR-3.2 | Allocation | Generate train recommendations (ranked list with scores) | P0 |
| FR-3.3 | Allocation | Display justifications (3+ reasons per recommendation) | P0 |
| FR-3.4 | Allocation | Confirm allocation (one-click assign) | P0 |
| FR-3.5 | Allocation | Filter trains by criteria (fleet, depot, km range) | P1 |
| FR-4.1 | Balancing | Visualize km distribution (histogram or box plot) | P0 |
| FR-4.2 | Balancing | Rank trains by delta (sorted list: most over/under) | P0 |
| FR-4.3 | Balancing | Show fleet average reference line | P0 |
| FR-4.4 | Balancing | Identify outliers (highlight >15% deviation) | P1 |
| FR-5.1 | Simulation | Create what-if scenario (clone current state) | P0 |
| FR-5.2 | Simulation | Modify future allocations in scenario | P0 |
| FR-5.3 | Simulation | Run simulation (calculate projected impact) | P0 |
| FR-5.4 | Simulation | Compare scenarios side-by-side | P0 |
| FR-5.5 | Simulation | Apply scenario to live plan | P1 |
| FR-6.1 | Gantt | Display timeline view (4-week, trains as rows) | P1 |
| FR-6.2 | Gantt | Show maintenance blocks (color-coded PM1/PM2/HM) | P1 |
| FR-6.3 | Gantt | Enable drag-drop scheduling | P1 |
| FR-6.4 | Gantt | Validate constraints real-time | P1 |
| FR-6.5 | Gantt | Show depot capacity per week | P1 |
| FR-7.1 | Solver | Accept service request (ID, date, route profile) | P0 |
| FR-7.2 | Solver | Filter available trains (exclude unfit, in-maintenance) | P0 |
| FR-7.3 | Solver | Score by balancing criteria (prioritize under-running) | P0 |
| FR-7.4 | Solver | Consider dead running (factor proximity) | P1 |
| FR-7.5 | Solver | Generate justifications (3+ human-readable reasons) | P0 |
| FR-7.6 | Solver | Return ranked list (top 5 recommendations) | P0 |

### Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Category | Requirement | Target |
|----|----------|-------------|--------|
| NFR-1.1 | Performance | Allocation recommendation time | < 30 seconds |
| NFR-1.2 | Performance | Screen loading | < 3 seconds |
| NFR-1.3 | Performance | Map refresh | < 5 seconds |
| NFR-1.4 | Performance | Simulation response | < 10 seconds |
| NFR-1.5 | Performance | Database queries | < 500ms |
| NFR-2.1 | Scalability | Trains supported | 80-120 |
| NFR-2.2 | Scalability | Depots supported | 12-15 |
| NFR-2.3 | Scalability | Daily services | 200-300 |
| NFR-2.4 | Scalability | Concurrent users | 10-20 |
| NFR-3.1 | Reliability | Demo stability | 15 min no crash |
| NFR-3.2 | Reliability | Data consistency | No conflicts |
| NFR-3.3 | Reliability | Fallback screens | Available |
| NFR-4.1 | Usability | Allocation flow | < 2 min, < 5 clicks |
| NFR-4.2 | Usability | Learning curve | 5 min orientation |
| NFR-4.3 | Usability | Domain terminology | 100% EN 15380 |

### Additional Requirements (from Architecture)

| ID | Category | Requirement |
|----|----------|-------------|
| AR-1 | Infrastructure | Docker Compose development environment |
| AR-2 | Infrastructure | Monorepo structure (apps/web, apps/api, solver/) |
| AR-3 | Database | PostgreSQL 16 with defined schema |
| AR-4 | Database | Seed data (100-120 trains, 12-15 depots, 200-300 services) |
| AR-5 | Backend | Fastify API server with routes per module |
| AR-6 | Backend | Redis caching with defined TTLs |
| AR-7 | Solver | Python FastAPI with OR-Tools CP-SAT |
| AR-8 | Frontend | Next.js 14 App Router structure |
| AR-9 | Frontend | @zazcart/toolkit for state management |
| AR-10 | Frontend | shadcn/ui component library |
| AR-11 | Resilience | Solver fallback (simple scoring if timeout) |
| AR-12 | Resilience | Error boundaries with retry |

---

### FR Coverage Map

| Epic | Functional Requirements Covered | NFRs Addressed |
|------|--------------------------------|----------------|
| Epic 1 | AR-1 to AR-12 | NFR-3.1, NFR-3.3 |
| Epic 2 | FR-1.1, FR-1.2, FR-1.3, FR-1.4, FR-1.5 | NFR-1.2, NFR-4.3 |
| Epic 3 | FR-2.1, FR-2.2, FR-2.3, FR-2.4, FR-2.5 | NFR-1.3 |
| Epic 4 | FR-3.1 to FR-3.5, FR-7.1 to FR-7.6 | NFR-1.1, NFR-4.1 |
| Epic 5 | FR-4.1 to FR-4.4, FR-5.1 to FR-5.5 | NFR-1.4 |
| Epic 6 | FR-6.1 to FR-6.5 | NFR-4.2 |

---

## Epic List

| Epic | Title | Primary Persona | Priority | Stories |
|------|-------|-----------------|----------|---------|
| 1 | Project Foundation & Infrastructure | Developer | P0 | 6 |
| 2 | Dashboard & KPIs | Gabriel (Manager) | P0 | 5 |
| 3 | Map & Fleet Visualization | Diana (Dispatcher) | P0 | 5 |
| 4 | Allocation with Recommendations | Diana (Dispatcher) | P0 | 6 |
| 5 | Km Balancing & Simulation | Paul (Planner) | P0 | 6 |
| 6 | Maintenance Gantt | Paul (Planner) | P1 | 5 |

---

## Epic 1: Project Foundation & Infrastructure

**Goal:** Establish the technical foundation enabling all feature development, including monorepo structure, database schema, API skeleton, and Python solver service.

**Covers:** AR-1 to AR-12, NFR-3.1, NFR-3.3

---

### Story 1.1: Setup Monorepo Structure

As a **developer**,
I want **a properly structured monorepo with all required packages**,
So that **I can develop frontend, backend, and solver in a coordinated way**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** an empty project directory
**When** the scaffolding script completes
**Then** the following structure exists:
```
mms/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ package.json (workspaces config)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ apps/web/ (Next.js 14)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ apps/api/ (Fastify)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ packages/types/ (shared TypeScript)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ solver/ (Python)
в””в”Җв”Җ docker-compose.yml
```
**And** `bun install` succeeds without errors
**And** TypeScript compilation passes for all packages

---

### Story 1.2: Configure Database Schema

As a **developer**,
I want **the complete PostgreSQL schema with migrations**,
So that **all entities are properly modeled and queryable**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** PostgreSQL 16 is running via Docker
**When** migrations are applied
**Then** the following tables exist with correct relationships:
- `fleets` (id, name, manufacturer, pm1/pm2/hm intervals)
- `depots` (id, name, code, lat/lon, capacity_tracks, capacity_manhours)
- `trains` (id, train_id, fleet_id FK, base_depot_id FK, current_km, fitness_status)
- `services` (id, service_code, origin, destination, distance_km, times)
- `allocations` (id, service_id FK, train_id FK, date, status, justifications JSONB)
- `mileage_history` (id, train_id FK, recorded_date, km_value)
- `maintenance_schedules` (id, train_id FK, depot_id FK, type, date, status)
**And** all indexes are created for performance
**And** FK constraints enforce data integrity

---

### Story 1.3: Create Seed Data Generator

As a **developer**,
I want **realistic seed data for demos**,
So that **the system demonstrates with believable railway data**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** an empty database with schema applied
**When** the seed script runs
**Then** the database contains:
- 4-5 fleets (e.g., Metro Series 2200, Intercity Series 3400)
- 12-15 depots with Portuguese city names and coordinates
- 100-120 trains distributed across fleets with varied mileage (Вұ30% from average)
- 200-300 daily services with realistic routes
- 6 months of mileage history showing km progression
**And** km variance across fleet is between 15-20% (to demonstrate balancing need)
**And** 5-10 trains are marked as outliers (>25% deviation)

---

### Story 1.4: Implement Fastify API Skeleton

As a **developer**,
I want **a working Fastify server with route stubs**,
So that **frontend can start integration early**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the API server starts successfully
**When** health check endpoint is called
**Then** `GET /health` returns `{ status: 'ok', timestamp: ISO date }`
**And** the following route files exist with stub implementations:
- `/api/dashboard/*` (summary, depot-capacity)
- `/api/trains/*` (list, details, history)
- `/api/depots/*` (list)
- `/api/services/*` (pending)
- `/api/allocations/*` (recommend, create)
- `/api/simulation/*` (create, run, compare)
**And** each stub returns appropriate mock data
**And** CORS is configured for `localhost:3000`

---

### Story 1.5: Setup Python Solver Service

As a **developer**,
I want **a FastAPI solver service with OR-Tools installed**,
So that **recommendation engine development can begin**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** Python 3.11 environment with requirements installed
**When** the solver service starts
**Then** `GET /health` returns `{ status: 'ok', ortools_version: 'x.x' }`
**And** `POST /recommend` endpoint exists (returns mock response initially)
**And** `POST /simulate` endpoint exists (returns mock response initially)
**And** response includes `solver_time_ms` field
**And** service runs on port 8000

---

### Story 1.6: Configure Docker Compose Environment

As a **developer**,
I want **one-command local development startup**,
So that **I can quickly reset and run the complete system**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** Docker and Docker Compose are installed
**When** `docker-compose up` is executed
**Then** the following services start:
- PostgreSQL 16 on port 5432
- Redis 7 on port 6379
**And** database is initialized with schema
**And** `docker-compose down -v && docker-compose up` resets to clean state
**And** `bun run seed` populates demo data after services are up

---

## Epic 2: Dashboard & KPIs

**Goal:** Provide Gabriel (Fleet Manager) with an executive overview showing fleet health, mileage variance, and actionable alerts at a glance.

**Covers:** FR-1.1 to FR-1.5, NFR-1.2, NFR-4.3

**Primary Persona:** Gabriel (Fleet Manager)

---

### Story 2.1: Display Fleet Summary KPIs

As a **Fleet Manager**,
I want **to see total trains, available count, maintenance count, and km variance at a glance**,
So that **I understand fleet health in seconds**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Dashboard page loads
**When** data fetches from `/api/dashboard/summary`
**Then** four KPI cards are displayed:
- Total Trains: shows count (e.g., "112")
- Available: shows count and percentage (e.g., "98 (87%)")
- In Maintenance: shows count (e.g., "14")
- Km Variance: shows percentage (e.g., "18%")
**And** each card uses EN 15380 terminology (not "Vehicle" or "Asset")
**And** page loads in < 3 seconds (NFR-1.2)

---

### Story 2.2: Show Variance Traffic-Light Indicator

As a **Fleet Manager**,
I want **the variance KPI to show green/yellow/red status**,
So that **I immediately know if mileage distribution is healthy**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Dashboard displays the Km Variance KPI
**When** variance is вүӨ10%
**Then** the indicator shows green color and checkmark icon
**When** variance is between 10-20%
**Then** the indicator shows yellow/amber color and warning icon
**When** variance is >20%
**Then** the indicator shows red color and alert icon
**And** hovering shows tooltip: "Fleet mileage variance: X% (target: вүӨ10%)"

---

### Story 2.3: Display Depot Capacity Cards

As a **Fleet Manager**,
I want **to see each depot's current occupancy**,
So that **I can identify capacity bottlenecks**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Dashboard page loads
**When** depot data fetches from `/api/dashboard/depot-capacity`
**Then** depot cards are displayed in a grid (2-4 columns)
**And** each card shows:
- Depot name and code
- Occupancy bar (current/max tracks)
- Percentage used with color coding (green <70%, yellow 70-90%, red >90%)
**And** clicking a depot card navigates to depot detail view

---

### Story 2.4: Show Maintenance Alerts

As a **Fleet Manager**,
I want **to see upcoming maintenance events**,
So that **I'm aware of planned capacity reductions**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Dashboard page loads
**When** there are maintenance events scheduled in the next 7 days
**Then** an alerts panel shows:
- Count of upcoming PM1, PM2, HM events
- List of next 5 events with train ID, type, date, depot
**And** clicking an alert navigates to the Maintenance Gantt view
**And** if no upcoming maintenance, shows "No scheduled maintenance in next 7 days"

---

### Story 2.5: Enable Drill-Down Navigation

As a **Fleet Manager**,
I want **to click any element to see details**,
So that **I can investigate issues without navigating menus**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Dashboard is displayed
**When** I click on "Available" KPI
**Then** I navigate to Fleet list filtered by status=fit
**When** I click on "In Maintenance" KPI
**Then** I navigate to Maintenance Gantt view
**When** I click on "Km Variance" KPI
**Then** I navigate to Balancing view with distribution chart
**When** I click on a depot card
**Then** I navigate to Fleet list filtered by depot
**And** all navigation maintains context (no page reload flash)

---

## Epic 3: Map & Fleet Visualization

**Goal:** Provide Diana (Dispatcher) with a real-time visual map showing train positions, depot locations, and quick access to train details.

**Covers:** FR-2.1 to FR-2.5, NFR-1.3

**Primary Persona:** Diana (Dispatcher)

---

### Story 3.1: Display Train Positions on Map

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **to see all active trains on an interactive map**,
So that **I understand current fleet distribution**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Map page loads
**When** train position data fetches from `/api/trains`
**Then** a map centered on Portugal displays
**And** each train appears as a marker at its lat/lon position
**And** trains without position show at their base depot location
**And** map renders in < 2 seconds

---

### Story 3.2: Color-Code Markers by Status

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **trains color-coded by operational status**,
So that **I quickly identify available trains**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Map displays train markers
**When** a train has fitness_status='fit' and no active service
**Then** marker is green (Available)
**When** a train has an active allocation today
**Then** marker is blue (In-Service)
**When** a train has fitness_status='unfit' or scheduled maintenance
**Then** marker is red (Maintenance)
**And** a legend shows the three status colors
**And** filter toggles allow showing/hiding each status

---

### Story 3.3: Show Depot Locations

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **depot locations visible on the map**,
So that **I understand the maintenance network**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Map page loads
**When** depot data fetches from `/api/depots`
**Then** each depot appears as a distinct icon (not train marker)
**And** depot icon shows capacity usage (color-coded pie or bar)
**And** hovering depot shows tooltip: "Name | Tracks: X/Y | Manhours: Z available"
**And** depots can be toggled on/off via map controls

---

### Story 3.4: Enable Train Selection

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **to click a train marker and see details**,
So that **I can assess train suitability quickly**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Map displays train markers
**When** I click a train marker
**Then** a slide-out panel or popup card shows:
- Train ID (e.g., UTE-3400-012)
- Fleet type
- Current km and variance from average
- Fitness status
- Base depot
- Next scheduled maintenance (type, date, km)
**And** card includes "View Details" link to full train page
**And** card includes "Allocate" button (if train is available)

---

### Story 3.5: Auto-Refresh Positions

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **train positions to update automatically**,
So that **I see near-real-time fleet state**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Map page is open
**When** 30 seconds pass
**Then** train positions refresh automatically
**And** markers animate smoothly to new positions (no flicker)
**And** a "Last updated: HH:MM:SS" indicator shows refresh time
**And** manual "Refresh Now" button is available
**And** refresh completes in < 5 seconds (NFR-1.3)

---

## Epic 4: Allocation with Recommendations

**Goal:** Enable Diana (Dispatcher) to allocate trains to services efficiently using AI-powered recommendations with visible justifications.

**Covers:** FR-3.1 to FR-3.5, FR-7.1 to FR-7.6, NFR-1.1, NFR-4.1

**Primary Persona:** Diana (Dispatcher)

---

### Story 4.1: List Pending Services

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **to see all services needing train assignment**,
So that **I can prioritize my allocation work**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Allocation page loads
**When** data fetches from `/api/services/pending`
**Then** a list/table shows pending services with columns:
- Service Code (e.g., IC 501)
- Origin вҶ’ Destination
- Distance (km)
- Departure time
- Status (Unassigned/Assigned)
**And** list is sorted by departure time ascending
**And** I can filter by date using a date picker
**And** clicking a service selects it for recommendation

---

### Story 4.2: Generate Train Recommendations

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **the system to recommend suitable trains ranked by score**,
So that **I make better allocation decisions faster**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** I have selected a pending service
**When** I click "Recommend"
**Then** the system calls `/api/allocations/recommend`
**And** within 30 seconds (NFR-1.1), a ranked list of 5 trains appears
**And** each recommendation shows:
- Train ID
- Score (0-100)
- Km delta from average (e.g., "-12%")
- Dead running distance
**And** recommendations are sorted by score descending
**And** if solver times out, fallback simple ranking is shown with notice

---

### Story 4.3: Display Justifications

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **to see why each train is recommended**,
So that **I trust the system's suggestions**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** recommendations are displayed
**When** I expand or hover a recommendation
**Then** I see 3+ justification reasons, such as:
- "Km 12% below fleet average (balancing priority)"
- "Service ends 5km from Porto depot"
- "PM1 scheduled in 3 days at destination depot"
- "Route compatible with train aptitude"
**And** each justification is a complete, human-readable sentence
**And** justifications use EN 15380 terminology

---

### Story 4.4: Confirm Allocation

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **to assign a recommended train with one click**,
So that **I complete allocations efficiently**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** I see the recommended trains list
**When** I click "Assign" on a recommendation
**Then** the system calls `POST /api/allocations`
**And** success toast shows "Train [ID] assigned to [Service]"
**And** the service moves from Pending to Assigned list
**And** the train's status updates on the Map
**And** entire flow completes in < 2 minutes, < 5 clicks (NFR-4.1)

---

### Story 4.5: Filter Trains by Criteria

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **to filter available trains by fleet, depot, or km range**,
So that **I can find specific trains when needed**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** I am on the Allocation page
**When** I open the filter panel
**Then** I can filter by:
- Fleet type (multi-select dropdown)
- Base depot (multi-select dropdown)
- Km range (min/max slider or inputs)
- Fitness status (fit only / show all)
**And** filters apply immediately to train list
**And** active filters are shown as chips that can be removed

---

### Story 4.6: Implement Solver Recommendation Engine

As a **system**,
I want **the Python solver to calculate optimal train assignments**,
So that **recommendations maximize mileage balancing**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** a `POST /recommend` request with service and candidates
**When** the OR-Tools CP-SAT solver runs
**Then** the response includes:
- Top 5 ranked trains by optimization score
- 3+ justification strings per train
- Dead running km calculation
- Solver execution time in ms
**And** solver respects constraints:
1. Exclude unfit trains (hard constraint)
2. Exclude trains with overlapping allocations (hard constraint)
3. Prioritize under-running trains (soft constraint, weight: high)
4. Minimize dead running (soft constraint, weight: medium)
5. Prefer trains ending near maintenance depot (soft constraint, weight: low)
**And** solver completes within 30 seconds or returns partial results

---

## Epic 5: Km Balancing & Simulation

**Goal:** Enable Paul (Planner) to visualize fleet mileage distribution, identify imbalances, and simulate allocation strategies to optimize balancing.

**Covers:** FR-4.1 to FR-4.4, FR-5.1 to FR-5.5, NFR-1.4

**Primary Persona:** Paul (Maintenance Planner)

---

### Story 5.1: Visualize Km Distribution

As a **Planner**,
I want **to see the km distribution across the fleet as a chart**,
So that **I understand the mileage spread**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Analysis/Balancing page loads
**When** distribution data fetches from `/api/balancing/distribution`
**Then** a histogram or box plot displays showing:
- X-axis: km ranges (buckets)
- Y-axis: number of trains
- Fleet average line clearly marked
- Target range highlighted (Вұ10% of average)
**And** chart is interactive (hover shows train count per bucket)
**And** I can filter by fleet type

---

### Story 5.2: Rank Trains by Delta

As a **Planner**,
I want **a sorted list showing trains by km deviation**,
So that **I quickly identify over/under-running trains**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Analysis/Balancing page loads
**When** train ranking is displayed
**Then** a sortable table shows:
- Train ID
- Current km
- Fleet average km
- Delta (km and %)
- Status indicator (over/under/balanced)
**And** default sort is by absolute delta descending (worst first)
**And** clicking a train row shows details or navigates to train page
**And** top 5 over-running and top 5 under-running are highlighted

---

### Story 5.3: Identify Outliers

As a **Planner**,
I want **outlier trains visually highlighted**,
So that **I focus attention on problem trains**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the distribution chart is displayed
**When** trains deviate >15% from fleet average
**Then** their histogram bar or point is highlighted in distinct color
**And** outlier trains are listed in a separate "Outliers" panel
**And** each outlier shows delta and recommended action:
- Over-running: "Reduce high-km allocations"
- Under-running: "Prioritize for upcoming services"

---

### Story 5.4: Create What-If Scenario

As a **Planner**,
I want **to create a simulation scenario**,
So that **I can test allocation strategies without affecting live data**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** I am on the Simulation section
**When** I click "New Scenario"
**Then** a dialog asks for scenario name
**And** clicking "Create" calls `POST /api/simulation/create`
**And** a sandbox copy of current allocations is created
**And** I enter "scenario mode" with visual indicator
**And** changes in scenario mode don't affect production data

---

### Story 5.5: Modify and Run Simulation

As a **Planner**,
I want **to modify allocations in a scenario and see projected impact**,
So that **I can evaluate strategies before committing**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** I am in scenario mode
**When** I reassign trains to different services
**Then** changes are tracked in the scenario
**And** clicking "Run Simulation" calls `POST /api/simulation/:id/run`
**And** within 10 seconds (NFR-1.4), results show:
- Projected variance after 30 days
- Projected dead running total
- Change from current state (better/worse indicators)
**And** I can run multiple iterations with different changes

---

### Story 5.6: Compare Scenarios

As a **Planner**,
I want **to compare scenarios side-by-side**,
So that **I choose the best strategy**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** I have created multiple scenarios
**When** I select 2-3 scenarios to compare
**Then** a comparison view shows:
- Metrics in columns (variance, dead running, cost impact)
- Best value highlighted green in each row
- Chart overlay showing distribution difference
**And** I can select a scenario and click "Apply to Live"
**And** applying scenario updates the production allocation plan

---

## Epic 6: Maintenance Gantt

**Goal:** Enable Paul (Planner) to visualize and schedule preventive maintenance using an interactive Gantt timeline with conflict validation.

**Covers:** FR-6.1 to FR-6.5, NFR-4.2

**Primary Persona:** Paul (Maintenance Planner)

---

### Story 6.1: Display Timeline View

As a **Planner**,
I want **to see maintenance schedules on a Gantt timeline**,
So that **I understand the maintenance calendar**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Schedule/Gantt page loads
**When** maintenance data fetches
**Then** a Gantt chart displays:
- Y-axis: Train IDs (rows)
- X-axis: 4-week timeline (days as columns)
- Horizontal scroll for extended view
- Today line clearly marked
**And** I can zoom in/out (week view, 2-week view, 4-week view)
**And** I can filter trains by fleet or depot

---

### Story 6.2: Show Maintenance Blocks

As a **Planner**,
I want **maintenance events as color-coded blocks**,
So that **I distinguish maintenance types**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Gantt timeline is displayed
**When** trains have scheduled maintenance
**Then** blocks appear on the timeline:
- PM1: Light blue
- PM2: Dark blue
- HM: Red
- Overhaul: Purple
**And** block width corresponds to duration (days)
**And** hovering block shows:
- Maintenance type
- Scheduled date(s)
- Depot
- Km at maintenance
**And** legend shows color meanings

---

### Story 6.3: Enable Drag-Drop Scheduling

As a **Planner**,
I want **to drag maintenance blocks to different dates**,
So that **I can adjust schedules interactively**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Gantt displays maintenance blocks
**When** I drag a maintenance block to a new date
**Then** the block moves visually following cursor
**And** drop zones highlight valid/invalid dates
**And** dropping on valid date updates the schedule
**And** system calls API to persist change
**And** undo option available for 10 seconds after change

---

### Story 6.4: Validate Constraints Real-Time

As a **Planner**,
I want **immediate feedback if a schedule change violates constraints**,
So that **I avoid creating invalid schedules**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** I am dragging a maintenance block
**When** I hover over a date that would cause conflict
**Then** the drop zone shows red indicator
**And** tooltip explains the conflict:
- "Depot at capacity on this date"
- "Outside maintenance window tolerance"
- "Overlaps with another maintenance"
**And** if I drop on invalid date, change is rejected with explanation
**And** valid changes show green confirmation

---

### Story 6.5: Show Depot Capacity per Week

As a **Planner**,
I want **to see aggregate depot capacity usage**,
So that **I avoid overloading maintenance facilities**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Gantt page loads
**When** I view the capacity panel
**Then** a heatmap or bar chart shows:
- Each depot as a row
- Each week as a column
- Color intensity indicates utilization (green < 70%, yellow 70-90%, red > 90%)
**And** clicking a cell shows trains scheduled at that depot that week
**And** if any depot/week is at 100%, warning icon appears

---

## Appendix: Story Dependencies

```
Epic 1 (Foundation)
   в””в”Җв”Җ All other epics depend on Epic 1 completion

Epic 2 (Dashboard) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
                        в”ңв”Җв”Җ Epic 4 (Allocation) uses train data
Epic 3 (Map) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

Epic 4 (Allocation)
   в””в”Җв”Җ Requires Solver (Story 4.6)

Epic 5 (Simulation)
   в””в”Җв”Җ Requires Balancing data (depends on Epic 4)

Epic 6 (Gantt)
   в””в”Җв”Җ Independent after Epic 1
```

---

## Sprint Recommendation

| Sprint | Epics | Focus |
|--------|-------|-------|
| Sprint 1 | Epic 1 (all), Epic 2 (2.1-2.2) | Foundation + Dashboard core |
| Sprint 2 | Epic 2 (2.3-2.5), Epic 3 | Dashboard complete + Map |
| Sprint 3 | Epic 4 (all) | Allocation + Solver |
| Sprint 4 | Epic 5 (all) | Balancing + Simulation |
| Sprint 5 | Epic 6 (all) | Gantt |
| Sprint 6 | Integration, Polish, Demo prep | End-to-end testing |

---

*Document generated by BMAD Epics Workflow*
*Date: 2025-12-28*

# Implementation Readiness Assessment Report

**Date:** 2025-12-28
**Project:** MMS - Metro Mileage Management System

---

## 1. Document Inventory

### Documents Assessed

| Document | File | Size | Modified |
|----------|------|------|----------|
| PRD | prd.md | 27KB | Dec 28 06:06 |
| Architecture | architecture.md | 23KB | Dec 28 06:12 |
| Epics & Stories | epics.md | 29KB | Dec 28 06:18 |
| UX Design | ux-design-specification.md | 27KB | Dec 28 06:09 |

### Document Status

- **Duplicates Found:** None
- **Missing Documents:** None
- **Format:** All documents in whole (single-file) format

---

## 2. PRD Analysis

### Functional Requirements Extracted

#### FR-1: Dashboard Module
| ID | Requirement | Priority |
|----|-------------|----------|
| FR-1.1 | Display fleet summary KPIs | P0 |
| FR-1.2 | Show variance indicator with traffic-light | P0 |
| FR-1.3 | Display depot capacity cards | P1 |
| FR-1.4 | Show maintenance alerts | P1 |
| FR-1.5 | Enable drill-down navigation | P0 |

#### FR-2: Map Module
| ID | Requirement | Priority |
|----|-------------|----------|
| FR-2.1 | Display train positions on map | P0 |
| FR-2.2 | Color-code by status | P0 |
| FR-2.3 | Show depot locations | P1 |
| FR-2.4 | Enable train selection | P0 |
| FR-2.5 | Auto-refresh positions | P1 |

#### FR-3: Allocation Module
| ID | Requirement | Priority |
|----|-------------|----------|
| FR-3.1 | List pending services | P0 |
| FR-3.2 | Generate train recommendations | P0 |
| FR-3.3 | Display justifications | P0 |
| FR-3.4 | Confirm allocation | P0 |
| FR-3.5 | Filter trains by criteria | P1 |

#### FR-4: Balancing Module
| ID | Requirement | Priority |
|----|-------------|----------|
| FR-4.1 | Visualize km distribution | P0 |
| FR-4.2 | Rank trains by delta | P0 |
| FR-4.3 | Show fleet average | P0 |
| FR-4.4 | Identify outliers | P1 |

#### FR-5: Simulation Module
| ID | Requirement | Priority |
|----|-------------|----------|
| FR-5.1 | Create what-if scenario | P0 |
| FR-5.2 | Modify future allocations | P0 |
| FR-5.3 | Run simulation | P0 |
| FR-5.4 | Compare scenarios | P0 |
| FR-5.5 | Apply scenario | P1 |

#### FR-6: Maintenance Gantt Module
| ID | Requirement | Priority |
|----|-------------|----------|
| FR-6.1 | Display timeline view | P1 |
| FR-6.2 | Show maintenance blocks | P1 |
| FR-6.3 | Enable drag-drop | P1 |
| FR-6.4 | Validate constraints | P1 |
| FR-6.5 | Show depot capacity | P1 |

#### FR-7: Recommendation Engine (Solver)
| ID | Requirement | Priority |
|----|-------------|----------|
| FR-7.1 | Accept service request | P0 |
| FR-7.2 | Filter available trains | P0 |
| FR-7.3 | Score by balancing criteria | P0 |
| FR-7.4 | Consider dead running | P1 |
| FR-7.5 | Generate justifications | P0 |
| FR-7.6 | Return ranked list | P0 |

**Total Functional Requirements: 33** (P0: 22, P1: 11)

### Non-Functional Requirements Extracted

#### NFR-1: Performance
| ID | Requirement | Target |
|----|-------------|--------|
| NFR-1.1 | Allocation recommendation time | < 30 seconds |
| NFR-1.2 | Screen loading | < 3 seconds |
| NFR-1.3 | Map refresh | < 5 seconds |
| NFR-1.4 | Simulation response | < 10 seconds |
| NFR-1.5 | Database queries | < 500ms |

#### NFR-2: Scalability
| ID | Requirement | Target |
|----|-------------|--------|
| NFR-2.1 | Trains supported | 80-120 |
| NFR-2.2 | Depots supported | 12-15 |
| NFR-2.3 | Daily services | 200-300 |
| NFR-2.4 | Concurrent users | 10-20 |

#### NFR-3: Reliability
| ID | Requirement | Target |
|----|-------------|--------|
| NFR-3.1 | Demo stability | 15 min no crash |
| NFR-3.2 | Data consistency | No conflicts |
| NFR-3.3 | Fallback screens | Available |

#### NFR-4: Usability
| ID | Requirement | Target |
|----|-------------|--------|
| NFR-4.1 | Allocation flow | < 2 min, < 5 clicks |
| NFR-4.2 | Learning curve | 5 min orientation |
| NFR-4.3 | Domain terminology | 100% EN 15380 |

**Total Non-Functional Requirements: 15**

### Additional Requirements Found

**Domain Terminology Constraint:**
- All UI labels must use EN 15380 railway terminology

**Constraint Priority (Solver Must Respect):**
1. Train Fitness - Unfit trains never assigned (hard constraint)
2. Maintenance Windows - Scheduled maintenance blocks availability
3. Resource Limits - Man-hours, track slots per depot
4. Km Balancing - Optimize within above constraints

**Data Requirements:**
- Core entities: trains, fleets, depots, services, allocations
- Seed data: 100-120 trains, 12-15 depots, 200-300 services/day, 6 months history

### PRD Completeness Assessment

| Aspect | Status | Notes |
|--------|--------|-------|
| Functional Requirements | вң… Complete | 33 FRs across 7 modules |
| Non-Functional Requirements | вң… Complete | 15 NFRs in 4 categories |
| User Journeys | вң… Complete | 4 detailed journeys with personas |
| Domain Model | вң… Complete | Entity relationships defined |
| Data Requirements | вң… Complete | Schema and seed data specified |
| API Endpoints | вң… Complete | 11 endpoints defined |
| Success Criteria | вң… Complete | User, Business, Technical metrics |

# Implementation Readiness Assessment Report

**Date:** 2025-12-28
**Project:** MMS - Metro Mileage Management System

---

## 1. Document Inventory

### Documents Assessed

| Document | File | Size | Modified |
|----------|------|------|----------|
| PRD | prd.md | 27KB | Dec 28 06:06 |
| Architecture | architecture.md | 23KB | Dec 28 06:12 |
| Epics & Stories | epics.md | 29KB | Dec 28 06:18 |
| UX Design | ux-design-specification.md | 27KB | Dec 28 06:09 |

### Document Status

- **Duplicates Found:** None
- **Missing Documents:** None
- **Format:** All documents in whole (single-file) format

---

## 2. PRD Analysis

### Functional Requirements Extracted

#### FR-1: Dashboard Module
| ID | Requirement | Priority |
|----|-------------|----------|
| FR-1.1 | Display fleet summary KPIs | P0 |
| FR-1.2 | Show variance indicator with traffic-light | P0 |
| FR-1.3 | Display depot capacity cards | P1 |
| FR-1.4 | Show maintenance alerts | P1 |
| FR-1.5 | Enable drill-down navigation | P0 |

#### FR-2: Map Module
| ID | Requirement | Priority |
|----|-------------|----------|
| FR-2.1 | Display train positions on map | P0 |
| FR-2.2 | Color-code by status | P0 |
| FR-2.3 | Show depot locations | P1 |
| FR-2.4 | Enable train selection | P0 |
| FR-2.5 | Auto-refresh positions | P1 |

#### FR-3: Allocation Module
| ID | Requirement | Priority |
|----|-------------|----------|
| FR-3.1 | List pending services | P0 |
| FR-3.2 | Generate train recommendations | P0 |
| FR-3.3 | Display justifications | P0 |
| FR-3.4 | Confirm allocation | P0 |
| FR-3.5 | Filter trains by criteria | P1 |

#### FR-4: Balancing Module
| ID | Requirement | Priority |
|----|-------------|----------|
| FR-4.1 | Visualize km distribution | P0 |
| FR-4.2 | Rank trains by delta | P0 |
| FR-4.3 | Show fleet average | P0 |
| FR-4.4 | Identify outliers | P1 |

#### FR-5: Simulation Module
| ID | Requirement | Priority |
|----|-------------|----------|
| FR-5.1 | Create what-if scenario | P0 |
| FR-5.2 | Modify future allocations | P0 |
| FR-5.3 | Run simulation | P0 |
| FR-5.4 | Compare scenarios | P0 |
| FR-5.5 | Apply scenario | P1 |

#### FR-6: Maintenance Gantt Module
| ID | Requirement | Priority |
|----|-------------|----------|
| FR-6.1 | Display timeline view | P1 |
| FR-6.2 | Show maintenance blocks | P1 |
| FR-6.3 | Enable drag-drop | P1 |
| FR-6.4 | Validate constraints | P1 |
| FR-6.5 | Show depot capacity | P1 |

#### FR-7: Recommendation Engine (Solver)
| ID | Requirement | Priority |
|----|-------------|----------|
| FR-7.1 | Accept service request | P0 |
| FR-7.2 | Filter available trains | P0 |
| FR-7.3 | Score by balancing criteria | P0 |
| FR-7.4 | Consider dead running | P1 |
| FR-7.5 | Generate justifications | P0 |
| FR-7.6 | Return ranked list | P0 |

**Total Functional Requirements: 33** (P0: 22, P1: 11)

### Non-Functional Requirements Extracted

#### NFR-1: Performance
| ID | Requirement | Target |
|----|-------------|--------|
| NFR-1.1 | Allocation recommendation time | < 30 seconds |
| NFR-1.2 | Screen loading | < 3 seconds |
| NFR-1.3 | Map refresh | < 5 seconds |
| NFR-1.4 | Simulation response | < 10 seconds |
| NFR-1.5 | Database queries | < 500ms |

#### NFR-2: Scalability
| ID | Requirement | Target |
|----|-------------|--------|
| NFR-2.1 | Trains supported | 80-120 |
| NFR-2.2 | Depots supported | 12-15 |
| NFR-2.3 | Daily services | 200-300 |
| NFR-2.4 | Concurrent users | 10-20 |

#### NFR-3: Reliability
| ID | Requirement | Target |
|----|-------------|--------|
| NFR-3.1 | Demo stability | 15 min no crash |
| NFR-3.2 | Data consistency | No conflicts |
| NFR-3.3 | Fallback screens | Available |

#### NFR-4: Usability
| ID | Requirement | Target |
|----|-------------|--------|
| NFR-4.1 | Allocation flow | < 2 min, < 5 clicks |
| NFR-4.2 | Learning curve | 5 min orientation |
| NFR-4.3 | Domain terminology | 100% EN 15380 |

**Total Non-Functional Requirements: 15**

### Additional Requirements Found

**Domain Terminology Constraint:**
- All UI labels must use EN 15380 railway terminology

**Constraint Priority (Solver Must Respect):**
1. Train Fitness - Unfit trains never assigned (hard constraint)
2. Maintenance Windows - Scheduled maintenance blocks availability
3. Resource Limits - Man-hours, track slots per depot
4. Km Balancing - Optimize within above constraints

**Data Requirements:**
- Core entities: trains, fleets, depots, services, allocations
- Seed data: 100-120 trains, 12-15 depots, 200-300 services/day, 6 months history

### PRD Completeness Assessment

| Aspect | Status | Notes |
|--------|--------|-------|
| Functional Requirements | вң… Complete | 33 FRs across 7 modules |
| Non-Functional Requirements | вң… Complete | 15 NFRs in 4 categories |
| User Journeys | вң… Complete | 4 detailed journeys with personas |
| Domain Model | вң… Complete | Entity relationships defined |
| Data Requirements | вң… Complete | Schema and seed data specified |
| API Endpoints | вң… Complete | 11 endpoints defined |
| Success Criteria | вң… Complete | User, Business, Technical metrics |

---

content.content:
1вҶ’---
     2вҶ’name: 'step-04-ux-alignment'
     3вҶ’description: 'Check for UX document and validate alignment with PRD and Architecture'
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’# Path Definitions
     6вҶ’workflow_path: '{project-root}/_bmad/bmm/workflows/3-solutioning/implementation-readiness'
     7вҶ’
     8вҶ’# File References
     9вҶ’thisStepFile: '{workflow_path}/steps/step-04-ux-alignment.md'
    10вҶ’nextStepFile: '{workflow_path}/steps/step-05-epic-quality-review.md'
    11вҶ’workflowFile: '{workflow_path}/workflow.md'
    12вҶ’outputFile: '{planning_artifacts}/implementation-readiness-report-{{date}}.md'
    13вҶ’---
    14вҶ’
    15вҶ’# Step 4: UX Alignment
    16вҶ’
    17вҶ’## STEP GOAL:
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’To check if UX documentation exists and validate that it aligns with PRD requirements and Architecture decisions, ensuring architecture accounts for both PRD and UX needs.
    20вҶ’
    21вҶ’## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):
    22вҶ’
    23вҶ’### Universal Rules:
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’- рҹӣ‘ NEVER generate content without user input
    26вҶ’- рҹ“– CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
    27вҶ’- рҹ”„ CRITICAL: When loading next step with 'C', ensure entire file is read
    28вҶ’- рҹ“Ӣ YOU ARE A FACILITATOR, not a content generator
    29вҶ’
    30вҶ’### Role Reinforcement:
    31вҶ’
    32вҶ’- вң… You are a UX VALIDATOR ensuring user experience is properly addressed
    33вҶ’- вң… UX requirements must be supported by architecture
    34вҶ’- вң… Missing UX documentation is a warning if UI is implied
    35вҶ’- вң… Alignment gaps must be documented
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’### Step-Specific Rules:
    38вҶ’
    39вҶ’- рҹҺҜ Check for UX document existence first
    40вҶ’- рҹҡ« Don't assume UX is not needed
    41вҶ’- рҹ’¬ Validate alignment between UX, PRD, and Architecture
    42вҶ’- рҹҡӘ Add findings to the output report
    43вҶ’
    44вҶ’## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:
    45вҶ’
    46вҶ’- рҹҺҜ Search for UX documentation
    47вҶ’- рҹ’ҫ If found, validate alignment
    48вҶ’- рҹ“– If not found, assess if UX is implied
    49вҶ’- рҹҡ« FORBIDDEN to proceed without completing assessment
    50вҶ’
    51вҶ’## UX ALIGNMENT PROCESS:
    52вҶ’
    53вҶ’### 1. Initialize UX Validation
    54вҶ’
    55вҶ’"Beginning **UX Alignment** validation.
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’I will:
    58вҶ’
    59вҶ’1. Check if UX documentation exists
    60вҶ’2. If UX exists: validate alignment with PRD and Architecture
    61вҶ’3. If no UX: determine if UX is implied and document warning"
    62вҶ’
    63вҶ’### 2. Search for UX Documentation
    64вҶ’
    65вҶ’Search patterns:
    66вҶ’
    67вҶ’- `{planning_artifacts}/*ux*.md` (whole document)
    68вҶ’- `{planning_artifacts}/*ux*/index.md` (sharded)
    69вҶ’- Look for UI-related terms in other documents
    70вҶ’
    71вҶ’### 3. If UX Document Exists
    72вҶ’
    73вҶ’#### A. UX вҶ” PRD Alignment
    74вҶ’
    75вҶ’- Check UX requirements reflected in PRD
    76вҶ’- Verify user journeys in UX match PRD use cases
    77вҶ’- Identify UX requirements not in PRD
    78вҶ’
    79вҶ’#### B. UX вҶ” Architecture Alignment
    80вҶ’
    81вҶ’- Verify architecture supports UX requirements
    82вҶ’- Check performance needs (responsiveness, load times)
    83вҶ’- Identify UI components not supported by architecture
    84вҶ’
    85вҶ’### 4. If No UX Document
    86вҶ’
    87вҶ’Assess if UX/UI is implied:
    88вҶ’
    89вҶ’- Does PRD mention user interface?
    90вҶ’- Are there web/mobile components implied?
    91вҶ’- Is this a user-facing application?
    92вҶ’

---
name: 'step-04-ux-alignment'
description: 'Check for UX document and validate alignment with PRD and Architecture'

# Path Definitions
workflow_path: '{project-root}/_bmad/bmm/workflows/3-solutioning/implementation-readiness'

# File References
thisStepFile: '{workflow_path}/steps/step-04-ux-alignment.md'
nextStepFile: '{workflow_path}/steps/step-05-epic-quality-review.md'
workflowFile: '{workflow_path}/workflow.md'
outputFile: '{planning_artifacts}/implementation-readiness-report-{{date}}.md'
---

# Step 4: UX Alignment

## STEP GOAL:

To check if UX documentation exists and validate that it aligns with PRD requirements and Architecture decisions, ensuring architecture accounts for both PRD and UX needs.

## MANDATORY EXECUTION RULES (READ FIRST):

### Universal Rules:

- рҹӣ‘ NEVER generate content without user input
- рҹ“– CRITICAL: Read the complete step file before taking any action
- рҹ”„ CRITICAL: When loading next step with 'C', ensure entire file is read
- рҹ“Ӣ YOU ARE A FACILITATOR, not a content generator

### Role Reinforcement:

- вң… You are a UX VALIDATOR ensuring user experience is properly addressed
- вң… UX requirements must be supported by architecture
- вң… Missing UX documentation is a warning if UI is implied
- вң… Alignment gaps must be documented

### Step-Specific Rules:

- рҹҺҜ Check for UX document existence first
- рҹҡ« Don't assume UX is not needed
- рҹ’¬ Validate alignment between UX, PRD, and Architecture
- рҹҡӘ Add findings to the output report

## EXECUTION PROTOCOLS:

- рҹҺҜ Search for UX documentation
- рҹ’ҫ If found, validate alignment
- рҹ“– If not found, assess if UX is implied
- рҹҡ« FORBIDDEN to proceed without completing assessment

## UX ALIGNMENT PROCESS:

### 1. Initialize UX Validation

"Beginning **UX Alignment** validation.

I will:

1. Check if UX documentation exists
2. If UX exists: validate alignment with PRD and Architecture
3. If no UX: determine if UX is implied and document warning"

### 2. Search for UX Documentation

Search patterns:

- `{planning_artifacts}/*ux*.md` (whole document)
- `{planning_artifacts}/*ux*/index.md` (sharded)
- Look for UI-related terms in other documents

### 3. If UX Document Exists

#### A. UX вҶ” PRD Alignment

- Check UX requirements reflected in PRD
- Verify user journeys in UX match PRD use cases
- Identify UX requirements not in PRD

#### B. UX вҶ” Architecture Alignment

- Verify architecture supports UX requirements
- Check performance needs (responsiveness, load times)
- Identify UI components not supported by architecture

### 4. If No UX Document

Assess if UX/UI is implied:

- Does PRD mention user interface?
- Are there web/mobile components implied?
- Is this a user-facing application?

If UX implied but missing: Add warning to report

### 5. Add Findings to Report

Append to {outputFile}:

```markdown
## UX Alignment Assessment

### UX Document Status

[Found/Not Found]

### Alignment Issues

[List any misalignments between UX, PRD, and Architecture]

### Warnings

[Any warnings about missing UX or architectural gaps]
```

### 6. Auto-Proceed to Next Step

After UX assessment complete, immediately load next step.

## PROCEEDING TO EPIC QUALITY REVIEW

UX alignment assessment complete. Loading next step for epic quality review.

---

## рҹҡЁ SYSTEM SUCCESS/FAILURE METRICS

### вң… SUCCESS:

- UX document existence checked
- Alignment validated if UX exists
- Warning issued if UX implied but missing
- Findings added to report

### вқҢ SYSTEM FAILURE:

- Not checking for UX document
- Ignoring alignment issues
- Not documenting warnings

    10вҶ’# UX Design Specification - MMS Metro Mileage Management System
    11вҶ’
    12вҶ’**Author:** MMS_PROGRAMMER
    13вҶ’**Date:** 2025-12-28
    14вҶ’
    15вҶ’---
    16вҶ’
    17вҶ’## 1. Design Philosophy
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’### 1.1 Core Principles
    20вҶ’
    21вҶ’| Principle | Application |
    22вҶ’|-----------|-------------|
    23вҶ’| **Domain Authenticity** | Use railway terminology (EN 15380), not generic software terms |
    24вҶ’| **Decision Support** | Every screen helps users make better decisions faster |
    25вҶ’| **Visual Trust** | Show data, not just results - users understand WHY |
    26вҶ’| **Progressive Disclosure** | Summary first, details on demand |
    27вҶ’| **Operational Efficiency** | < 5 clicks for critical tasks |
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’### 1.2 Design Mantra
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’> "The interface should feel like it was built by someone who has spent time in a railway control room."
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’---
    34вҶ’
    35вҶ’## 2. User Personas
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’### 2.1 Paul - The Maintenance Planner
    38вҶ’
    39вҶ’| Attribute | Value |
    40вҶ’|-----------|-------|
    41вҶ’| **Age** | 45 years |
    42вҶ’| **Daily System Usage** | 4-8 hours |
    43вҶ’| **Primary Device** | Desktop with large monitor |
    44вҶ’| **Key Tasks** | Schedule maintenance, balance workload, manage depot capacity |
    45вҶ’| **Pain Points** | Excel spreadsheets, lack of visibility, constant rework |
    46вҶ’| **Critical Need** | Gantt chart with drag-drop functionality |
    47вҶ’
    48вҶ’**Design Implications:**
    49вҶ’- Large data tables with sorting and filtering
    50вҶ’- Gantt view as primary planning interface
    51вҶ’- Keyboard shortcuts for power users
    52вҶ’- Dense information display (comfort with complexity)
    53вҶ’
    54вҶ’### 2.2 Diana - The Dispatcher
    55вҶ’
    56вҶ’| Attribute | Value |
    57вҶ’|-----------|-------|
    58вҶ’| **Age** | 38 years |
    59вҶ’| **Daily System Usage** | Continuous during shift |
    60вҶ’| **Primary Device** | Desktop with multiple monitors |
    61вҶ’| **Key Tasks** | Allocate trains to services in real-time |
    62вҶ’| **Pain Points** | Slow systems, lack of information for quick decisions |
    63вҶ’| **Critical Need** | Response time < 3 seconds |
    64вҶ’
    65вҶ’**Design Implications:**
    66вҶ’- Quick action panels, single-click confirmations
    67вҶ’- Map always visible on secondary monitor
    68вҶ’- Status at a glance (traffic light indicators)
    69вҶ’- Minimal navigation for common tasks
    70вҶ’
    71вҶ’### 2.3 Gabriel - The Fleet Manager
    72вҶ’
    73вҶ’| Attribute | Value |
    74вҶ’|-----------|-------|
    75вҶ’| **Age** | 52 years |
    76вҶ’| **Weekly System Usage** | 30-60 minutes |
    77вҶ’| **Primary Device** | Desktop or tablet |
    78вҶ’| **Key Tasks** | Monitor KPIs, make strategic decisions |
    79вҶ’| **Pain Points** | Scattered data, lack of drill-down capability |
    80вҶ’| **Critical Need** | Dashboard with executive view |
    81вҶ’
    82вҶ’**Design Implications:**
    83вҶ’- Large KPI cards, minimal text
    84вҶ’- Trend visualizations over time
    85вҶ’- Touch-friendly for tablet use
    86вҶ’- Export to PDF for presentations
    87вҶ’

   444вҶ’
   445вҶ’## 6. Interaction Patterns
   446вҶ’
   447вҶ’### 6.1 Critical Flows
   448вҶ’
   449вҶ’#### Allocate Train to Service (< 2 min, < 5 clicks)
   450вҶ’
   451вҶ’```
   452вҶ’1. Click "Allocation" in nav (1 click)
   453вҶ’2. Select pending service (1 click)
   454вҶ’3. Review recommendations
   455вҶ’4. Click "Assign" on recommended train (1 click)
   456вҶ’5. Confirm allocation (1 click)
   457вҶ’
   458вҶ’Total: 4 clicks, ~90 seconds
   459вҶ’```
   460вҶ’
   461вҶ’#### Identify and Resolve Imbalance (< 10 min)
   462вҶ’
   463вҶ’```
   464вҶ’1. Dashboard shows variance warning
   465вҶ’2. Click variance card вҶ’ Balancing screen
   466вҶ’3. Review distribution chart
   467вҶ’4. Click "New Simulation"
   468вҶ’5. Adjust allocations in scenario
   469вҶ’6. Click "Run Simulation"
   470вҶ’7. Compare results
   471вҶ’8. Click "Apply" if acceptable
   472вҶ’
   473вҶ’Total: 8 steps, ~8 minutes
   474вҶ’```
   475вҶ’
   476вҶ’### 6.2 Feedback Patterns
   477вҶ’
   478вҶ’| Action | Feedback |
   479вҶ’|--------|----------|
   480вҶ’| Button Click | Visual press state (scale 0.98) |
   481вҶ’| Data Loading | Skeleton placeholders |
   482вҶ’| Success | Toast notification (green) |
   483вҶ’| Error | Toast notification (red) + inline error |
   484вҶ’| Confirmation | Modal dialog for destructive actions |
   485вҶ’| Progress | Progress bar for long operations |
   486вҶ’
   487вҶ’### 6.3 Error Handling
   488вҶ’
   489вҶ’| Error Type | Handling |
   490вҶ’|------------|----------|
   491вҶ’| Network | Retry button + cached data indicator |
   492вҶ’| Validation | Inline error message below field |
   493вҶ’| Constraint Conflict | Dialog with resolution options |
   494вҶ’| Solver Timeout | Fallback to simpler recommendation |
   495вҶ’
   496вҶ’---
   497вҶ’
   498вҶ’## 7. Responsive Behavior
   499вҶ’
   500вҶ’### 7.1 Breakpoints
   501вҶ’
   502вҶ’| Breakpoint | Width | Layout |
   503вҶ’|------------|-------|--------|
   504вҶ’| Desktop XL | вүҘ1440px | Full layout, map side-by-side |
   505вҶ’| Desktop | вүҘ1024px | Standard layout |
   506вҶ’| Tablet | вүҘ768px | Stacked panels, map full-width |
   507вҶ’| Mobile | <768px | Single column, limited features |
   508вҶ’
   509вҶ’### 7.2 Mobile Considerations
   510вҶ’
   511вҶ’**Demo Context:** Primary usage is desktop/tablet. Mobile is not a priority.
   512вҶ’
   513вҶ’For tablet (Gabriel's use case):
   514вҶ’- Touch-friendly buttons (44px minimum)
   515вҶ’- Simplified navigation
   516вҶ’- Landscape orientation preferred
   517вҶ’- Focus on Dashboard and KPIs
   518вҶ’
   519вҶ’---
   520вҶ’
   521вҶ’## 8. Accessibility
   522вҶ’
   523вҶ’### 8.1 WCAG 2.1 AA Compliance
   524вҶ’
   525вҶ’| Requirement | Implementation |
   526вҶ’|-------------|----------------|
   527вҶ’| Color Contrast | 4.5:1 minimum for text |
   528вҶ’| Focus Indicators | 2px solid outline |
   529вҶ’| Screen Reader | ARIA labels on interactive elements |
   530вҶ’| Keyboard Nav | Tab order follows visual flow |
   531вҶ’
   532вҶ’### 8.2 Color Blindness
   533вҶ’
   534вҶ’All status indicators use:
   535вҶ’- Color + icon/symbol
   536вҶ’- Color + text label
   537вҶ’- Pattern differentiation where possible
   538вҶ’

   541вҶ’## 9. Performance Guidelines
   542вҶ’
   543вҶ’### 9.1 Loading Targets
   544вҶ’
   545вҶ’| Metric | Target |
   546вҶ’|--------|--------|
   547вҶ’| First Contentful Paint | < 1.5s |
   548вҶ’| Time to Interactive | < 3s |
   549вҶ’| Dashboard Load | < 2s |
   550вҶ’| Map Render | < 2s |
   551вҶ’| API Response Display | < 500ms |
   552вҶ’
   553вҶ’### 9.2 Optimization Strategies
   554вҶ’
   555вҶ’- Skeleton loading for data tables
   556вҶ’- Map tile caching
   557вҶ’- Lazy load off-screen components
   558вҶ’- Virtual scrolling for long lists
   559вҶ’- Debounce search inputs
   560вҶ’

   569вҶ’| Map with "live" trains | Animated markers, realistic positions |
   570вҶ’| Interactive simulation | Immediate visual feedback |
   571вҶ’| Justification clarity | Well-formatted reason cards |
   572вҶ’| Domain authenticity | Correct terminology throughout |
   573вҶ’
   574вҶ’### 10.2 Fallback Screens
   575вҶ’
   576вҶ’For demo stability, prepare:
   577вҶ’- Static dashboard screenshot (if API fails)
   578вҶ’- Pre-computed recommendation (if solver times out)
   579вҶ’- Cached map tiles (if network slow)
   580вҶ’
   581вҶ’### 10.3 Demo Flow Optimization
   582вҶ’
   583вҶ’**15-Minute Demo Path:**
   584вҶ’1. Dashboard overview (2 min)
   585вҶ’2. Map drill-down to train (2 min)
   586вҶ’3. Allocation with justification (4 min)
   587вҶ’4. Balancing visualization (3 min)
   588вҶ’5. Simulation comparison (3 min)
   589вҶ’6. Q&A (1 min)
   590вҶ’
   591вҶ’---

# UX Design Specification - MMS Metro Mileage Management System

**Author:** MMS_PROGRAMMER
**Date:** 2025-12-28

---

## 1. Design Philosophy

### 1.1 Core Principles

| Principle | Application |
|-----------|-------------|
| **Domain Authenticity** | Use railway terminology (EN 15380), not generic software terms |
| **Decision Support** | Every screen helps users make better decisions faster |
| **Visual Trust** | Show data, not just results - users understand WHY |
| **Progressive Disclosure** | Summary first, details on demand |
| **Operational Efficiency** | < 5 clicks for critical tasks |

### 1.2 Design Mantra

> "The interface should feel like it was built by someone who has spent time in a railway control room."

---

## 2. User Personas

### 2.1 Paul - The Maintenance Planner

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| **Age** | 45 years |
| **Daily System Usage** | 4-8 hours |
| **Primary Device** | Desktop with large monitor |
| **Key Tasks** | Schedule maintenance, balance workload, manage depot capacity |
| **Pain Points** | Excel spreadsheets, lack of visibility, constant rework |
| **Critical Need** | Gantt chart with drag-drop functionality |

**Design Implications:**
- Large data tables with sorting and filtering
- Gantt view as primary planning interface
- Keyboard shortcuts for power users
- Dense information display (comfort with complexity)

### 2.2 Diana - The Dispatcher

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| **Age** | 38 years |
| **Daily System Usage** | Continuous during shift |
| **Primary Device** | Desktop with multiple monitors |
| **Key Tasks** | Allocate trains to services in real-time |
| **Pain Points** | Slow systems, lack of information for quick decisions |
| **Critical Need** | Response time < 3 seconds |

**Design Implications:**
- Quick action panels, single-click confirmations
- Map always visible on secondary monitor
- Status at a glance (traffic light indicators)
- Minimal navigation for common tasks

### 2.3 Gabriel - The Fleet Manager

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| **Age** | 52 years |
| **Weekly System Usage** | 30-60 minutes |
| **Primary Device** | Desktop or tablet |
| **Key Tasks** | Monitor KPIs, make strategic decisions |
| **Pain Points** | Scattered data, lack of drill-down capability |
| **Critical Need** | Dashboard with executive view |

**Design Implications:**
- Large KPI cards, minimal text
- Trend visualizations over time
- Touch-friendly for tablet use
- Export to PDF for presentations

---

## 3. Information Architecture

### 3.1 Navigation Structure

```

**KPI Card Specification:**

| Element | Specification |
|---------|---------------|
| Size | 120px Г— 100px minimum |
| Primary Number | 48px, Bold |
| Label | 14px, Gray |
| Secondary | 12px, Color-coded |
| Click Action | Navigate to detail view |

**Color Coding (Traffic Light):**
- рҹҹў Green: Normal (Variance вүӨ 10%, Capacity вүӨ 70%)
- рҹҹЎ Yellow: Warning (Variance 10-20%, Capacity 70-90%)
- рҹ”ҙ Red: Alert (Variance > 20%, Capacity > 90%)

**Drag-Drop Behavior:**
1. User clicks and holds maintenance block
2. Ghost follows cursor
3. Valid drop zones highlight in green
4. Invalid zones (conflicts) highlight in red
5. On drop: validate constraints, show confirmation
6. If conflict: show resolution options

---

## 5. Visual Design System

### 5.1 Color Palette

**Primary Colors:**
| Color | Hex | Usage |
|-------|-----|-------|
| Navy Blue | #1E3A5F | Primary brand, headers |
| Steel Blue | #4A6FA5 | Secondary, links |
| White | #FFFFFF | Backgrounds |
| Light Gray | #F3F4F6 | Alt backgrounds |

**Status Colors:**
| Status | Hex | Usage |
|--------|-----|-------|
| Success | #22C55E | Available, confirmed, on-target |
| Warning | #EAB308 | Caution, approaching threshold |
| Error | #EF4444 | Alert, over threshold, unfit |
| Info | #3B82F6 | In-service, informational |
| Neutral | #9CA3AF | Inactive, disabled |

### 5.2 Typography

| Element | Font | Size | Weight |
|---------|------|------|--------|
| H1 (Page Title) | Inter | 24px | Bold |
| H2 (Section) | Inter | 20px | Semibold |
| H3 (Card Title) | Inter | 16px | Semibold |
| Body | Inter | 14px | Regular |
| Small/Caption | Inter | 12px | Regular |
| KPI Number | Inter | 48px | Bold |
| Table Header | Inter | 12px | Semibold |
| Table Cell | Inter | 14px | Regular |

### 5.3 Spacing System

Using 4px base unit:
- xs: 4px
- sm: 8px
- md: 16px
- lg: 24px
- xl: 32px
- 2xl: 48px

### 5.4 Component Library

Using **shadcn/ui** components:
- Button (primary, secondary, ghost, destructive)
- Card (with header, content, footer)
- Table (sortable, filterable)
- Dialog (modal, confirmation)
- Select/Dropdown
- Tabs
- Badge (status indicators)
- Tooltip
- Progress

---

## 6. Interaction Patterns

### 6.1 Critical Flows

#### Allocate Train to Service (< 2 min, < 5 clicks)

```
1. Click "Allocation" in nav (1 click)
2. Select pending service (1 click)
3. Review recommendations
4. Click "Assign" on recommended train (1 click)
5. Confirm allocation (1 click)

Total: 4 clicks, ~90 seconds
```

#### Identify and Resolve Imbalance (< 10 min)

```
1. Dashboard shows variance warning
2. Click variance card вҶ’ Balancing screen
3. Review distribution chart
4. Click "New Simulation"
5. Adjust allocations in scenario
6. Click "Run Simulation"
7. Compare results
8. Click "Apply" if acceptable

Total: 8 steps, ~8 minutes
```

### 6.2 Feedback Patterns

| Action | Feedback |
|--------|----------|
| Button Click | Visual press state (scale 0.98) |
| Data Loading | Skeleton placeholders |
| Success | Toast notification (green) |
| Error | Toast notification (red) + inline error |
| Confirmation | Modal dialog for destructive actions |
| Progress | Progress bar for long operations |

### 6.3 Error Handling

| Error Type | Handling |
|------------|----------|
| Network | Retry button + cached data indicator |
| Validation | Inline error message below field |
| Constraint Conflict | Dialog with resolution options |
| Solver Timeout | Fallback to simpler recommendation |

---

## 7. Responsive Behavior

### 7.1 Breakpoints

| Breakpoint | Width | Layout |
|------------|-------|--------|
| Desktop XL | вүҘ1440px | Full layout, map side-by-side |
| Desktop | вүҘ1024px | Standard layout |
| Tablet | вүҘ768px | Stacked panels, map full-width |
| Mobile | <768px | Single column, limited features |

### 7.2 Mobile Considerations

**Demo Context:** Primary usage is desktop/tablet. Mobile is not a priority.

For tablet (Gabriel's use case):
- Touch-friendly buttons (44px minimum)
- Simplified navigation
- Landscape orientation preferred
- Focus on Dashboard and KPIs

---

## 8. Accessibility

### 8.1 WCAG 2.1 AA Compliance

| Requirement | Implementation |
|-------------|----------------|
| Color Contrast | 4.5:1 minimum for text |
| Focus Indicators | 2px solid outline |
| Screen Reader | ARIA labels on interactive elements |
| Keyboard Nav | Tab order follows visual flow |

### 8.2 Color Blindness

All status indicators use:
- Color + icon/symbol
- Color + text label
- Pattern differentiation where possible

---

## 9. Performance Guidelines

### 9.1 Loading Targets

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| First Contentful Paint | < 1.5s |
| Time to Interactive | < 3s |
| Dashboard Load | < 2s |
| Map Render | < 2s |
| API Response Display | < 500ms |

### 9.2 Optimization Strategies

- Skeleton loading for data tables
- Map tile caching
- Lazy load off-screen components
- Virtual scrolling for long lists
- Debounce search inputs

---

## 10. Demo-Specific Considerations

### 10.1 Wow Moments

| Moment | Design Element |
|--------|----------------|
| Map with "live" trains | Animated markers, realistic positions |
| Interactive simulation | Immediate visual feedback |
| Justification clarity | Well-formatted reason cards |
| Domain authenticity | Correct terminology throughout |

### 10.2 Fallback Screens

For demo stability, prepare:
- Static dashboard screenshot (if API fails)
- Pre-computed recommendation (if solver times out)
- Cached map tiles (if network slow)

### 10.3 Demo Flow Optimization

**15-Minute Demo Path:**
1. Dashboard overview (2 min)
2. Map drill-down to train (2 min)
3. Allocation with justification (4 min)
4. Balancing visualization (3 min)
5. Simulation comparison (3 min)
6. Q&A (1 min)

---

*Document generated by BMAD UX Design Workflow*
*Date: 2025-12-28*

    61вҶ’в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ       в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ      в”Ӯ

    64вҶ’в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ    trains     в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  OR-Tools CP-SAT  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ      в”Ӯ
    65вҶ’в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ    depots     в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Recommendation   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ      в”Ӯ
    66вҶ’в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   services    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ     Engine        в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ      в”Ӯ
    67вҶ’в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  allocations  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ӯ      в”Ӯ
    68вҶ’в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ӯ       в”Ӯ         вҶ‘ HTTP          в”Ӯ      в”Ӯ
    69вҶ’в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ       в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ      в”Ӯ

    77вҶ’### 1.3 Key Architectural Decisions
    78вҶ’
    79вҶ’| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
    80вҶ’|----------|--------|-----------|
    81вҶ’| Frontend Framework | Next.js 14 (App Router) | SSR for initial load, React ecosystem |
    82вҶ’| Styling | Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui | Rapid development, consistent design |
    83вҶ’| Backend Framework | Fastify | Performance, TypeScript support |
    84вҶ’| Database | PostgreSQL 16 | Reliable, JSON support, demo-proven |
    85вҶ’| Solver | Python + OR-Tools | Best-in-class optimization, justifications |
    86вҶ’| State Management | @zazcart/toolkit | Avoids React hook pitfalls |
    87вҶ’| API Style | REST | Simplicity for demo scope |
    88вҶ’
